validation_results.name: Tests
validation_results.command: pnpm --filter @intelliflow/api exec vitest run modules/lead (163 incl. 18 new audit-logging)

validation_results.name: Lint
validation_results.command: eslint --max-warnings 0 on the 3 touched files + prettier --check (format-check) clean

gate_results.log_path: TypeScript / Tests / Lint / Build all exit 0. Re-confirmed in the full pre-ship run at the final SHA.

Every changed line in lead.router.ts is an audit call covered by a matching assertion in lead.router.audit-logging.test.ts (16 procedure tests). The single shared .catch handler (logLeadAuditFailure) is covered by 2 resilience tests that reject logAction/logBulkOperation. No istanbul-ignore (L2). diff-coverage gate run in pre-ship at the final SHA.
node scripts/codex-review.mjs → Verdict PASS, 0 findings (0 unwaived) across the 3 in-scope files.

Audit logging uses the base ctx.prisma singleton (getAuditLogger) with an explicit tenantId per entry — it does NOT bypass tenant isolation of business data (matches opportunity router IFC-281). The IFC-237 T-011 static guard was updated to recognise getAuditLogger(ctx.prisma) as the only allowed base-client consumer; a raw ctx.prisma.lead.* would still fail the guard.

AC-4: a rejected audit write never alters or blocks a procedure result. Proven by 2 tests (create + bulkDelete) that reject the logger and assert the normal response is still returned. All 16 calls route rejections to logLeadAuditFailure.
No mocked/simulated runtime data. Audit entries carry real actorId/tenantId/resourceId from the request context and real before/after/metadata from the operation result.

kpi_results.actual: 16/21 procedures instrumented = all 15 mutations (create, update, delete, setStarred, qualify, convert, convertToDeal, scoreWithAI, addNote, logActivity, bulkScore, bulkConvert, bulkUpdateStatus, bulkArchive, bulkDelete) + getById single-record READ. The 5 collection/aggregate reads (list, stats, getHotLeads, getReadyForQualification, filterOptions) are excluded by design — high-volume reads already governed by tenant isolation + RBAC, no single identifiable data subject (spec §3). 100% of state-changing and GDPR-relevant operations are covered.
100% — delete, bulkDelete, convert, convertToDeal all emit audit entries (the task's named GDPR ops; no lead export procedure exists). getById additionally logs single-record PII access.
18 new audit tests + full lead suite (163) green; every changed line exercised; diff-coverage gate run in pre-ship.

definition_of_done_items.criterion: All lead procedures emit audit entries
definition_of_done_items.evidence: 16/21 instrumented: 15 mutations + getById READ. The 5 excluded reads are aggregate/collection endpoints (spec §3 rationale). 100% of mutating + GDPR-relevant + single-record-access procedures covered.

create/update carry afterState; bulk ops carry successCount/failureCount + the affected ids + operation metadata; convert/convertToDeal carry conversion metadata (createAccount/opportunityId). delete/bulkDelete record actor + resourceId(s) (no after-state — record removed).

definition_of_done_items.criterion: Audit entries queryable via audit API
definition_of_done_items.evidence: Uses the existing getAuditLogger facade → writeEntry into the audit tables read by queryComprehensive/getResourceAuditTrail/getActorAuditTrail (apps/api/src/security/audit/queries.ts). No new storage.

100% of GDPR-relevant ops (delete, bulkDelete, convert, convertToDeal) + single-record read (getById) carry actor/action/entityType/entityId/tenantId/metadata. Tenant isolation preserved (T-011 guard updated).
18 new tests (one per instrumented procedure + 2 fire-and-forget resilience) + full lead suite 163 green; diff-coverage in pre-ship.

environment.git_commit: 10920d28c (base origin/main@c56600d4d; attestation + CSV flip ride feat/ifc-240)

notes:
IFC-240 Lead Router Audit Logging — instruments apps/api/src/modules/lead/lead.router.ts with the existing audit infrastructure (getAuditLogger facade), replicating the IFC-281 opportunity-router pattern to close the GDPR Art. 30 / forensic-trail gap on the leads vertical.

SCOPE: 15 mutations emit logAction/logBulkOperation; getById emits a READ access entry. All calls are fire-and-forget via a shared logLeadAuditFailure handler — a rejected audit write never alters or blocks a procedure result (AC-4, proven by 2 resilience tests). Bulk ops log the actually-affected (successful) ids + success/failure counts. The 5 collection/aggregate reads are excluded by design (spec §3).

CO-DEPENDENT (rides this branch per the Co-Dependent Changes rule): the IFC-237 T-011 static guard in lead.router.security.test.ts forbade any ctx.prisma usage; updated to recognise getAuditLogger(ctx.prisma) as the only allowed base-client consumer (singleton audit writer with explicit tenantId per entry — tenant-safe), keeping the invariant's teeth against raw ctx.prisma.lead.* access.

VALIDATION: 4/4 (TypeScript, Tests, Lint, Build). codex-review PASS (0 findings). Full pre-ship run at the final SHA before push. Lane E head — unblocks IFC-255 (contact router audit logging, same pattern).
